reasoning_summary: Diagnosing live run 20260421_170056_bf16_gemm_v1_13d24542 at 31.240 ms after node_c direction `Trim PTX 64x64 Microkernel Register Footprint Back To 3-CTA Class`. The run regressed by 6.549 ms versus the previous measured run, and the profiler makes the culprit unusually clear: `runs/20260421_170056_bf16_gemm_v1_13d24542/ncu_details_page.csv` shows the hot-band PTX kernel `bf16_gemm_v1_tensor_core_fixed_hot_band_128x128_ptx_microkernel<(int)452>` consuming 42.27 ms of profiled duration with 241 registers/thread, while the peeled 64x384 kernel and the small tail kernel are both below 1 ms. That means the new interleaved helper added at `src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:820-921` did not shrink the live set in practice; it made the dominant kernel even harder to resident-hide, leaving occupancy at 16.6%, tensor pipe at 37.48%, barrier at 9.8%, and mio_throttle at 15.39% despite very low DRAM throughput (12.92%). Dir_01 therefore recommends deleting or bypassing the failed interleaved microkernel and returning the hot-band PTX path to a more compact row-pair accumulator schedule before touching staging or tiling. Dir_02 keeps the same hot-band surface but ports its cp.async cadence toward the existing 2-K Pg2s stage schedule to attack barrier and mio_throttle after the register problem is under control. Dir_03 keeps the user-guided 256x128 / 64x64 warp-tile reopen alive as the only high-ceiling structural branch, but it stays third because the current run is dominated by a brand-new localized regression rather than a fundamental ceiling on the older 128x128 family.

family_audit.reason: The persistent 256x128 block / 64x64 warp guidance still maps to dir_03, but the live run is dominated by a fresh hot-band PTX microkernel regression. Repair the localized regression before reopening the broader tiling branch.
Wide 16-byte global-to-shared copies are already in place (`kAsyncCopyElems = 8`), and DRAM throughput is only 12.92%. The next move is not a coalescing rewrite.
More A-panel reuse is plausible through the 256x128 reopen, but the current regression is explained first by register growth on the existing hot-band kernel rather than missing shared-memory reuse.
Barrier (9.8%) and mio_throttle (15.39%) remain materially high on the dominant hot-band path, so a Pg2s / wait-group retime stays in the top three as dir_02.
Neither the structured summary nor the raw details-page export surfaces a bank-conflict hotspot. Keep the existing skewed shared layout and defer padding/permutation work.
L2 throughput is only 24.67% and DRAM is 12.92%, so the kernel is not blocked on cache reach or swizzle quality before it is blocked on occupancy and synchronization.

This is the primary family for the current round, but the just-measured `col_step_interleaved` subfamily is rejected by evidence. The live run shows 241 registers/thread on the dominant hot-band PTX kernel, so the next move is to simplify or remove that failed live-range rewrite.
The file already contains a two-K-stage hot-band sibling, and the current stall mix says the PTX path still exposes the global-to-shared refill seam. Keep that as the second-ranked direction.
Adding more shared-to-register prefetch state would push in the wrong direction on a kernel already measured at 241 registers/thread. Do not add extra register buffering this round.

Stage depth is still a valid secondary lever, but only as a bounded hot-band cadence retime on the existing 128x128 surface. Any stage experiment must keep register and shared-memory growth under control.

directions.name: Replace The Failed Interleaved PTX 64x64 Hot-Band Microkernel With The Compact Row-Pair Path
directions.hypothesis: The newly added outer-col-step interleave helper at `src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:820-921` failed its own premise. Instead of shrinking the hot-band PTX live set, the measured run landed at 241 registers/thread and the hot-band kernel now dominates the NCU capture. Replacing that helper with the existing compact 64x64 PTX accumulate path (`ptx_wmma_accumulate_tile_set_64x64<false>`) or an equivalently scoped row-pair schedule should recover occupancy and most of the 6.55 ms regression without changing launch geometry, shared layout, or the row-group mapping.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register-pressure-driven occupancy collapse on the dominant hot-band PTX kernel: 241 registers/thread keeps the kernel at 2 CTAs/SM and leaves tensor utilization low despite unsaturated memory bandwidth.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:733-922 (generic 64x64 PTX accumulate helper, failed interleaved helper, and dispatch wrapper)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:2068-2185 (hot-band PTX store and loop callsite that now routes through the failed helper)
directions.risk: Moderate. The math path stays the same, but removing the interleaved helper can give back some of the intended B-fragment sharing or re-expose prior barrier pressure if the replacement is not kept compact.
ranking_notes: The current dir_01 implementation is directly falsified by measurement: the code comment claims a path back toward the 3-CTA class, but the live run reports 241 registers/thread and a much slower runtime.
The hot-band PTX kernel is the problem surface. `ncu_details_page.csv` shows it consuming 42.27 ms of profiled duration, while the peeled 64x384 and tail kernels are both below 1 ms.
This is the best upside/risk tradeoff because it attacks a fresh localized regression before adding more staging state or reopening a broader family.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the hot-band PTX kernel still reports more than about 220 registers/thread after the simplification, or if runtime fails to return to the sub-27 ms class while barrier worsens materially.
expected_local_changes: Delete or bypass `ptx_wmma_accumulate_col_step_interleaved_64x64_ptx_microkernel` and stop routing the hot-band PTX kernel through that helper.
Reuse the existing compact 64x64 PTX accumulate helper or a similarly scoped row-pair schedule so A/B fragment lifetimes stay local instead of being spread across the templated outer column loop.
Leave grouped-row mapping, cp.async wide loads, and the current hot-band launch shape unchanged for the first validation pass.

directions.name: Port The PTX Hot-Band Path To The Existing 2-K Pg2s Stage Schedule
directions.hypothesis: The current PTX hot-band kernel still waits and synchronizes every single K tile. Barrier is 9.8%, mio_throttle is 15.39%, and long_scoreboard is 4.64%, all on a kernel whose DRAM throughput is only 12.92%. The file already contains a two-K-stage 128x128 hot-band sibling at `src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:1813-1956` that batches two K tiles per stage. Porting the PTX hot-band math path onto that Pg2s schedule should reduce barrier cadence and refill exposure without changing the 128x128 CTA footprint.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Wait-group, barrier, and refill cadence on the dominant hot-band path rather than raw global-memory bandwidth.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:2089-2185 (current PTX hot-band shared buffers, prolog, and steady-state loop)
directions.risk: Moderate-high. This touches the prolog, steady-state loop, and drain logic together, and it can worsen register or shared-memory pressure if the PTX path is not kept bounded.
ranking_notes: This directly maps to the measured barrier and mio_throttle signals and to the user guidance around Async Copy, Pg2s, and Stage.
The code already contains a neighboring two-K-stage schedule, so the direction has a concrete implementation template instead of being a blank-slate pipeline rewrite.
It ranks behind dir_01 because layering more stage state onto a 241-register kernel is riskier than first removing the fresh register-live-range regression.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if barrier fails to drop below the current 9.8% class, or if shared/register growth keeps the hot-band PTX kernel pinned at the current 2-CTA occupancy class.
expected_local_changes: Reuse the existing two-K-stage hot-band prolog and refill cadence as the template for the PTX 128x128 branch.
Batch two A/B async-copy loads per stage so the PTX hot-band path synchronizes once per two K tiles instead of once per single K tile.
Adjust wait-group placement so the PTX path keeps more work in flight without growing the launch surface beyond the current 128x128 hot-band region.

directions.name: Reopen 256x128 64x64-Warp Hot-Band Tiling For The Dominant Surface
directions.hypothesis: The raw details-page export shows the 128x128 PTX hot-band kernel dominating profiled time, while the 64x384 peeled path and the tail path are small. The source already contains `FixedHotBandTile256x128`, and the persistent human guidance explicitly asks for 256x128 block tiling with 64x64 warp tiling and Right-Left-Right-Left register reuse. Reopening that family is the only broad structural move with enough ceiling to matter if the current 128x128 repair path plateaus.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Hot-band tiling and panel-reuse ceiling: the 128x128 hot-band surface is doing too much coordination per useful math tile and leaves too much work on the slowest profiled kernel.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:675-739 (generic 64x64 PTX accumulate helper already aligned with larger warp-tile reuse)
directions.risk: High. This is a broader structural reopen with known correctness and performance history. It touches writer ownership, export layout, and launch routing, and should not be the first recovery move after a localized regression.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness on all 3 dataset cases before trusting runtime
ranking_notes: This is the highest-ceiling family left because it directly targets the kernel that dominates profiled time and aligns with the user's tiling guidance.
It stays third because the current run is not evidence that the 128x128 family is fundamentally exhausted; it is evidence that one brand-new microkernel rewrite failed badly.
Only reopen this branch after the current hot-band PTX regression is repaired or cleanly shown to have no fast local fix.
directions.stop_condition: Stop immediately on any correctness failure, or if the first measured reopen stays in the previously bad 28-30 ms class instead of clearly beating the current 31.24 ms regression surface.
expected_local_changes: Rewire the hot-band launch surface so the dominant region uses the 256x128 / 64x64-warp family instead of the current 128x128 PTX microkernel.
Repair or restore writer-safe export ownership on the 256x128 path before trusting any runtime number.
Keep the 64x384 main path and the 64x96 tail unchanged so the experiment isolates the hot-band family itself.

notes: Human-guidance audit for this round: keep Register Reuse as the primary family, but reject the just-measured `col_step_interleaved` subfamily because the live run contradicts its design claim. Keep Async Copy / Pg2s / Stage accepted as the secondary family on the same 128x128 PTX surface, and keep 256x128 Tiling / Data Reuse alive only as the high-ceiling reopen once the current localized regression has been unwound.
